assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 5: 'Selected mode: feature-branch'. Lines 7-15 detail the rationale based on atomicity: coordinated schema migrations, breaking API changes, and tightly coupled feature components. Line 13 quotes the feature's non-functional requirements: 'All changes must land together: merging the migration without the code changes would break all advisory queries.'

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-feature-branch.md lines 9-10: '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch'. This is task 1 of 8, the first task in the plan. summary-comment.md line 13 confirms: '| 1 | Create feature branch TC-9005 from main | trustify-backend | main | Bookend (create-branch) |'

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-feature-branch.md lines 9-10: '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch'. This is task 8 of 8, the last task in the plan. summary-comment.md line 20 confirms: '| 8 | Merge feature branch TC-9005 to main | trustify-backend | main | Bookend (merge-branch) |'

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: All six intermediate tasks have Target Branch TC-9005: task-2-database-migration.md line 8: 'TC-9005'; task-3-update-seaorm-entities.md line 8: 'TC-9005'; task-4-update-advisory-service-endpoints.md line 8: 'TC-9005'; task-5-update-ingestion-pipeline.md line 8: 'TC-9005'; task-6-update-integration-tests.md line 8: 'TC-9005'; task-7-update-documentation.md line 8: 'TC-9005'.

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main

All non-documentation intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency. Documentation tasks may list implementation tasks as dependencies instead of the create-branch bookend task, since documentation depends on implementation completion
assertion_results.evidence: Non-documentation intermediate tasks all list Task 1 as a dependency: task-2 line 57: 'Depends on: Task 1'; task-3 line 48: 'Depends on: Task 1'; task-4 line 56: 'Depends on: Task 1'; task-5 line 45: 'Depends on: Task 1'; task-6 line 43: 'Depends on: Task 1'. Documentation task 7 (lines 35-39) lists implementation tasks 2-6 as dependencies instead of Task 1, which is permitted by the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all non-documentation intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-feature-branch.md lists all non-documentation intermediate tasks as dependencies: line 35: 'Task 2'; line 36: 'Task 3'; line 37: 'Task 4'; line 38: 'Task 5'; line 39: 'Task 6'. It also includes documentation task 7 (line 40), which does not contradict the requirement that all non-documentation tasks are listed.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks 1 and 8 are non-documentation tasks but lack required sections. Task 1 (task-1-create-feature-branch.md) is missing: Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes sections. Task 8 (task-8-merge-feature-branch.md) is missing: Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes sections. Implementation tasks 2-6 all have the required sections. Documentation task 7 has all required sections (Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements) and is correctly exempt from Files and Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 17: 'The workflow:feature-branch label will be applied to the feature issue TC-9005.' summary-comment.md lines 53-55: 'The workflow:feature-branch label is appended to the existing labels on TC-9005: ["ai-generated-jira", "workflow:feature-branch"]'

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: All 8 tasks have separate digest files with properly formatted sha256-md hashes: task-1-digest.md: sha256-md:818f679f3c73e88c5ece5c48ab008e62dc383340d6d7396aaa925fb2201e2060; task-2-digest.md: sha256-md:080eab6f8986532a13c4b8c8eab49a61d6d14f431f069aae5890125090ec8849; task-3-digest.md: sha256-md:54b6170fa22ca1e328e6730491ab115f664a439dc8620b4aab43265af5fd1b02; task-4-digest.md: sha256-md:0744ef02ef937fab8ff4cfbc1b873fffefe38e5d3b54038e98c26ae7ad95a0f2; task-5-digest.md: sha256-md:679266565650fb434d41f930b581e5fe22910055dd5092c31ec7d4cb248b433b; task-6-digest.md: sha256-md:87b85f232283993393cb8ff491db999eef7f215ccfea981219d04985a8820dff; task-7-digest.md: sha256-md:3f718c353eef0fcbc443c1e8010ada4028ca10b7c73095465eac7748ff266d15; task-8-digest.md: sha256-md:3030bf8e6ebeee54ed7009e2730fdb8da84f9c8024f0c0ac45acdc18c86f8512. All are 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by sha256-md:.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention enrichments follow the prescribed format. Task 2: 'Applies: task creates migration/src/m0002_advisory_status_enum/mod.rs matching the convention's Rust migration file scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies entity/src/advisory.rs matching the convention's Rust entity file scope.' Task 4 has four conventions with rationales referencing specific files (endpoints/list.rs, service/advisory.rs). Task 5 has two conventions referencing graph/advisory/mod.rs. Task 6 has one convention referencing tests/api/advisory.rs. No 'Not applicable' annotations appear anywhere. Bookend tasks (1, 8) and documentation task (7) correctly omit conventions since they have no code files matching any convention scope.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md lines 39-40: 'Priority: High — propagated from Feature TC-9005 to all created tasks. The Feature's priority is set and is not "Undefined", so it was included in additional_fields on each task.' Lines 44-49 show the additional_fields JSON with '"priority": {"name": "High"}'.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md lines 41-42: 'fixVersion: RHTPA 2.0.0 — propagated from Feature TC-9005 to all created tasks. The Feature has a non-empty fixVersions array, and no fixVersion scope setting exists in Jira Field Defaults (defaults to "both"), so fixVersions were included in additional_fields on each task.' Lines 44-49 confirm the JSON: '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 2.0.0"}]'. CLAUDE.md has no ### Jira Field Defaults section, so the default scope of 'both' correctly applies.

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists in the outputs directory and contains an '## Inherited Field Propagation' section (lines 37-50) documenting: Priority 'High' propagated from TC-9005 to all tasks; fixVersion 'RHTPA 2.0.0' propagated; the additional_fields JSON applied to each created task; and the workflow:feature-branch label applied to the feature issue.
